content.full_text:
A-82-151

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662                322-5660                  322-5901              322-6441                322-6444

September 2, 1982


Kirk Pessner
Dobbs & Nielsen
Suite 2500, The Alcoa Building
One Maritime Plaza
San Francisco 94111

Dear Mr. Pessner:

     Robert Stern has asked me to answer your letter dated
August 26, 1982, regarding the reporting of late contributions
pursuant to Gov. Code Section 84206(b) and late independent
expenditures pursuant to Gov. Code Section 84207(a).

     Gov. Code Sections 84206(b) and 84207(a) require that
late contributions and late independent expenditures be
reported by "mailgram, telegram or personal delivery" within
48 hours.

     You pointed out the problems associated with transmitting
information by mailgrams or telegrams through Western Union,
including the fact that occasionally Western Union fails to
transmit or deliver the mailgrams or telegrams within the
required 48 hours, and the fact that the sender has no source
of documentation that the information has been sent within
the required 48 hours, except the confirmation copy which is
received by the sender on the same day as the mailgram or
telegram is delivered to the filing officer.

     You have asked whether the use of Express Mail, Federal
Express or other overnight guaranteed delivery service would
satisfy the requirements of Gov. Code Sections 84206(b) and
84207(a).

     The staff has determined that the use of Express Mail,
Federal Express or other overnight guaranteed delivery service
is an acceptable method of transmittal which would serve the

Kirk Pessner
September 2, 1982
Page 2


purpose and intent of the requirements contained in Gov. Code
Sections 84206(b) and 84207(a).

       Thank you for your inquiry.  Please contact us if you
have any further questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Political Reform Consultant

FPPC

Aug 30 12:47 PM '82

DOBBS & NIELSEN
Attorneys and Counselors at Law

LOS ANGELES OFFICE
SUITE 309, 612 SOUTH FLOWER STREET
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90017
(213) 627-5055

A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS
SUITE 2500, THE ALCOA BUILDING
ONE MARITIME PLAZA
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94111

SACRAMENTO OFFICE
SUITE 550, 1225 EIGHTH STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 446-6752

(415) 362-1040

SAN DIEGO OFFICE
SUITE 4000, 4000 MACARTHUR BOULEVARD
NEWPORT BEACH, CALIFORNIA 92660
(714) 752-9318

August 26, 1982


Robert Stern, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
110 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Stern:

This letter requests advice from your office regarding reporting "late contributions and independent expenditures." Specifically, we request advice pursuant to the provisions of Government Code Sections 84206(b) and 84207(a) which provide that Late Contribution and Expenditure Reports shall be made "by mailgram, telegram, or personal delivery within 48 hours." This office seeks permission to file late contribution and independent expenditure notices by U.S. Postal Express Mail and/or Federal Express or other overnight guaranteed delivery service if sent in time to achieve delivery the next day, which day falls within 48 hours of receipt or delivery.

On several occasions, the notices of late contributions and expenditures that are dictated to Western Union operators for transmittal by telegram or mailgram, contain misspellings and sometimes inaccurate information, even though this office as a policy requests that the mailgram or telegram be read back. Occasionally, Western Union will fail to transmit a mailgram or telegram to a filing officer, particularly when there are several filing officers receiving the same message. Using the Western Union telephone service offers no proof that the filer has indeed telephoned the message, and our only source of documentation is the confirmation copy which is received on the same day that the mailgram or telegram is received by the filing official.

Express Mail and/or Federal Express or other overnight guaranteed delivery service does on the other hand 1) insure that the information reported is accurate, 2) may even be received earlier than Western Union (the Secretary of State can verify

Robert Stern, Esq.
August 26, 1982
Page 2


transmission problems on deadline days), and 3) provide a receipt
that the package containing the information has been sent and that
delivery is guaranteed by the next working day.  All these bene-
fits further the purposes of the Act and appeal to fall within the
definition of "personal delivery" if sent in time will certainly
comply with the 48-hour reporting requirement of the law.

     Please advise us at your earliest convenience whether
or not Express Mail and/or Federal Express sent under these
conditions is an acceptable procedure for filing Late Contribution
and Expenditure Reports.

                                        Kind regards,


                                        Kirk Allan Pessner
                                        Administrator
                                        Political Report/
                                        Research Unit

KAP:tjd

sections.question: Whether the use of Express Mail, Federal Express or other overnight guaranteed delivery service would satisfy the requirements of Gov. Code Sections 84206(b) and 84207(a) for reporting late contributions and late independent expenditures within 48 hours.
sections.conclusion: The staff has determined that the use of Express Mail, Federal Express or other overnight guaranteed delivery service is an acceptable method of transmittal which would serve the purpose and intent of the requirements contained in Gov. Code Sections 84206(b) and 84207(a).
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether Express Mail, Federal Express, or other overnight guaranteed delivery services satisfy California's requirement that late contributions and independent expenditures be reported within 48 hours, as an alternative to the statutory mailgram/telegram/personal delivery methods.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC staff concludes that Express Mail, Federal Express, and other overnight guaranteed delivery services are acceptable methods for reporting late contributions and independent expenditures, as they satisfy the purpose and intent of the 48-hour reporting requirement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated August 26, 1982) and the FPPC response letter (dated September 2, 1982). The response letter from Jeanne Pritchard provides clear conclusion approving alternative delivery methods. Minor handwriting signature present but legible header information.

QUESTION: Whether the use of Express Mail, Federal Express or other overnight guaranteed delivery service would satisfy the requirements of Gov. Code Sections 84206(b) and 84207(a) for reporting late contributions and late independent expenditures within 48 hours.

CONCLUSION: The staff has determined that the use of Express Mail, Federal Express or other overnight guaranteed delivery service is an acceptable method of transmittal which would serve the purpose and intent of the requirements contained in Gov. Code Sections 84206(b) and 84207(a).

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to a request regarding acceptable methods for reporting late political contributions and independent expenditures under California Government Code Sections 84206(b) and 84207(a). The FPPC approves the use of Express Mail, Federal Express, and similar overnight delivery services as alternatives to mailgrams and telegrams, provided they achieve delivery within the required 48-hour timeframe.
